title: Charmed Life - Chapter 9 (Fragment 2)
text:
He was just on the last bend when a door at the top opened. A brighter square of light shone on the wall at the head of the stairs, and a shadow stood in it that could only belong to Chrestomanci. No one else’s shadow could be so tall, with such a smooth head and such a lot of ruffles on its shirt front. Cat stopped.

“And let’s hope the wretched girl won’t try that again,” Chrestomanci said, out of sight above. He sounded a good deal more alert than usual, and rather angry.

Mr. Saunders’ voice, from farther away, said, “I’ve had about enough of her already, frankly. I suppose she’ll come to her senses soon. What possessed her to give away the source of her power like that?”

“Ignorance,” said Chrestomanci. “If I thought she had the least idea what she was doing, it would be the last thing she ever did in that line—or any other.”

“My back was to it,” said Mr. Saunders. “Which was it? Number five?”

“No. Number three, by the look of its hair. A revenant,” said Chrestomanci. “For which we must be thankful.” He began to come down the stairs. Cat was too scared to move. “I’ll have to get the Examining Board to revise their Elementary Magic Courses,” Chrestomanci called back as he came downstairs, “to include more theory. These hedge wizards push their good pupils straight on to advanced work without any proper grounding at all.” Saying this, Chrestomanci came down around the corner and saw Cat. “Oh, hello,” he said. “I’d no idea you were here. Like to come up and have a look at Michael’s workshop?”

Cat nodded. He did not dare do otherwise.

Chrestomanci seemed quite friendly, however, and so did Mr. Saunders when Chrestomanci ushered Cat into the room at the top of the stairs. “Hello, Eric,” he said in his cheerful way. “Have a look around. Does any of this mean anything to you?”

Cat shook his head. The room was round, like his own, but larger, and it was a regular magician’s workshop. That much he could see. He recognized the five-pointed star painted on the floor. The smell coming from the burning cresset hanging from the ceiling was the same smell that had hung about Coven Street, back in Wolvercote. But he had no idea of the use of the things set out on the various trestle tables. One table was crowded with torts and limbecks, some bubbling, some empty. A second was piled with books and scrolls. The third bench had signs chalked all over it and a mummified creature of some sort lying among the signs.

Cat’s eyes traveled over all this, and over more books crammed into shelves around the walls, and more shelves filled with jars of ingredients—big jars, like the ones in sweet shops. He realized Mr. Saunders worked in a big way. His scudding eyes raced over some of the labels on the huge jars: Newts’ Eyes, Gum Arabic, Elixir St. John’s Wort, Dragons’ blood (dried). The last jar was almost full of dark brown powder. Cat’s eyes went back to the mummified animal stretched among the signs chalked on the third table. Its feet had claws like a dog’s. It looked like a large lizard. But there seemed to be wings on its back. Cat was almost sure it had once been a small dragon.

vocabulary.definition: Quick to notice and respond to potential danger or problems.
vocabulary.example: He sounded a good deal more alert than usual.

vocabulary.definition: Giving off or reflecting more light.
vocabulary.example: A brighter square of light shone on the wall at the head of the stairs.

vocabulary.definition: Sharp hooked nails on the feet of some animals.
vocabulary.example: Its feet had claws like a dog’s.

vocabulary.definition: A metal cup or basket for holding a lighted wick or burning oil, used as a light source.
vocabulary.example: The smell coming from the burning cresset hanging from the ceiling was the same smell that had hung about Coven Street.

vocabulary.definition: A medicinal solution or potion, often with magical qualities.
vocabulary.example: His scudding eyes raced over some of the labels on the huge jars: Newts’ Eyes, Gum Arabic, Elixir St. John’s Wort.

vocabulary.definition: A committee responsible for overseeing examinations and standards.
vocabulary.example: I’ll have to get the Examining Board to revise their Elementary Magic Courses.

vocabulary.definition: A star with five points, often used as a symbol in magic.
vocabulary.example: He recognized the five-pointed star painted on the floor.

vocabulary.definition: In an open, honest, and direct manner.
vocabulary.example: I’ve had about enough of her already, frankly.

vocabulary.definition: Basic education or training in a subject.
vocabulary.example: These hedge wizards push their good pupils straight on to advanced work without any proper grounding at all.

vocabulary.definition: A natural gum consisting of the hardened sap of various species of the acacia tree, used in various preparations.
vocabulary.example: His scudding eyes raced over some of the labels on the huge jars: Newts’ Eyes, Gum Arabic, Elixir St. John’s Wort.

vocabulary.definition: Informal or lesser trained practitioners of magic, often working outside formal institutions.
vocabulary.example: These hedge wizards push their good pupils straight on to advanced work without any proper grounding at all.

vocabulary.definition: Lack of knowledge or information.
vocabulary.example: Ignorance, said Chrestomanci.

vocabulary.definition: Parts or elements that make up a mixture or compound, often used in cooking or potion-making.
vocabulary.example: More shelves filled with jars of ingredients—big jars, like the ones in sweet shops.

vocabulary.definition: Alchemy vessels or flasks used for the distillation of liquids.
vocabulary.example: One table was crowded with torts and limbecks, some bubbling, some empty.

vocabulary.definition: A room or place where a magician or wizard works and practices magic.
vocabulary.example: The room was round, like his own, but larger, and it was a regular magician’s workshop.

vocabulary.definition: Preserved from decay, typically by drying or embalming.
vocabulary.example: A mummified creature of some sort lying among the signs.

vocabulary.definition: An animal preserved by mummification, often used in magical or ritual contexts.
vocabulary.example: The last jar was almost full of dark brown powder. Cat’s eyes went back to the mummified animal stretched among the signs chalked on the third table.

vocabulary.definition: To have a particular quality or feeling completely control a person.
vocabulary.example: What possessed her to give away the source of her power like that?

vocabulary.definition: A person who has returned, especially supposedly from the dead.
vocabulary.example: Number three, by the look of its hair. A revenant.

vocabulary.definition: To reconsider and amend (something).
vocabulary.example: I’ll have to get the Examining Board to revise their Elementary Magic Courses.

vocabulary.definition: A strip of gathered or pleated fabric used as decoration on garments.
vocabulary.example: Such a lot of ruffles on its shirt front.

vocabulary.definition: Rolls of parchment or paper for writing or painting, often ancient.
vocabulary.example: A second was piled with books and scrolls.

vocabulary.definition: Moving fast in a rush or driven by the wind; used metaphorically about quick movements, here eyes.
vocabulary.example: His scudding eyes raced over some of the labels on the huge jars.

vocabulary.definition: A dark shape produced by a body coming between rays of light and a surface.
vocabulary.example: A shadow stood in it that could only belong to Chrestomanci.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling or showing gratitude.
vocabulary.example: For which we must be thankful.

translations.ru: посредства для дистилляции или перегонки (специфическое название сосуда)

vocabulary.definition: A type of flask or vessel used in alchemy or laboratory work (archaic or specialized term).
vocabulary.example: One table was crowded with torts and limbecks, some bubbling, some empty.

vocabulary.definition: Tables supported by trestles, often portable and used for work surfaces.
vocabulary.example: One table was crowded with torts and limbecks, some bubbling, some empty.

vocabulary.definition: To show or guide someone somewhere.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci ushered Cat into the room at the top of the stairs.

vocabulary.definition: Very bad or unpleasant; miserable.
vocabulary.example: And let’s hope the wretched girl won’t try that again.
